---
description: "Use when adding or modifying i18n translation strings in message bundles. Covers locale coverage, file format, and interpolation."
applyTo: "srv/_i18n/**"
---
# Internationalization (i18n) Guidelines

## Locale coverage
When adding or changing ANY string, update ALL 8 locale files:
- `messages.properties` (English — default/fallback)
- `messages_de.properties` (German)
- `messages_es.properties` (Spanish)
- `messages_fr.properties` (French)
- `messages_hi.properties` (Hindi)
- `messages_iw.properties` (Hebrew)
- `messages_ja.properties` (Japanese)
- `messages_pl.properties` (Polish)

## File format
Standard Java `.properties` format:
```properties
keyName=Value text here
keyWithParam=Hello {0}, welcome to {1}
```

- Keys are camelCase.
- No quotes around values.
- Parameters use `{0}`, `{1}`, etc. positional placeholders.
- Lines starting with `#` are comments.

## Usage in code
```js
const { getBundle } = require('../util/texts')
const bundle = getBundle(req)
const text = bundle.getText('keyName', [param0, param1])
```

## Do NOT
- Add a string to only one locale file — all 8 must be updated together.
- Use hardcoded strings in SVG output — always go through the text bundle.
- Change existing keys that are in use — add new keys instead to avoid breaking other locales.
